cases.prompt: The request is vague: a new internal tool idea with unclear depth. Decide the smallest Grill Me mode and stage map before asking Q1.
Align on the problem, target user, MVP, non-goals, acceptance checks, and what success looks like before any UI or backend work.
Managers need bulk approve across a table and drawer. We need to decide routes, states, permission gates, and recovery paths.

For appointment reminders, the bottom-nav entry, list-vs-calendar route, and first UI entry point are undecided; the real Reminders route does not exist yet, so the next move needs a current-design-system fallback mock before implementation.

A UI-flow stage has two route options. The user needs to see them in the real route, Storybook, Widgetbook, simulator, or localhost review surface, then save a ui-review-receipt with selected and rejected options before implementation.
The screen flow is clear, but users must choose between visual directions, tokens, type, density, component feel, and motion tone.
Before building a mock-data prototype, choose the prototype runtime, component convention, mock boundary, and preview command.
Build or plan the clickable local prototype with mock data, covered states, artifact paths, preview URL, and user approval.
Decide API, schema, auth, storage, realtime, deploy, migrations, observability, rollback, and backend boundaries.
Turn accepted decisions into vertical slices, task waves, acceptance criteria, verification, dependencies, and risk gates.
The interview is done. Synthesize session state, answer ledger, stage handoffs, PRODUCT.md, DESIGN.md, and write the canonical plan.md.
After compaction, resume from docs/planning/foo/session_state.md and plan_draft.md before asking the next Grill Me question.
The repo glossary and ADRs may conflict with fuzzy product terms. Check domain docs and capture confirmed vocabulary or ADR candidates.
Inside an already-active Grill Me stage, format the next question with one clear Q, meaning, why it matters, suggested default, options, and answer format.
